failure_surface: Repetitive SyntaxError: invalid syntax at line 1 during code execution, despite syntactically correct Python logic
root_cause: Non-standard control characters or formatting artifacts introduced at the start of code blocks prevented proper code parsing by the execution environment
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all code strings passed to execution tools are free of invisible Unicode characters, markdown artifact prefixes, or non-ASCII content that could corrupt the code stream before submission
evidence: Multiple consecutive attempts produced identical 'SyntaxError: invalid syntax' at Cell In[1], line 1 across both execute_python_code and write_file calls
Code content shown in parameters appears syntactically valid (standard imports, function definitions, loops)
Error persists across 4 separate submission attempts with slight code variations
Preceding content before function tags shows unusual character sequence (<tool_call>) indicating potential encoding corruption or improper tool wrapping

memory_items.title: Tool Invocation Code Hygiene
memory_items.lesson: Always validate code strings contain only standard ASCII/text characters before submitting to execution environments
memory_items.generalization: Control characters, emoji artifacts, or non-printing Unicode in code blocks will cause immediate parse failures regardless of logical correctness

memory_items.title: Execution Environment Error Patterns
memory_items.lesson: Recurring SyntaxError at line 1 across multiple attempts indicates input contamination rather than code logic errors
memory_items.generalization: When syntax errors appear instantly at line 1 with valid-looking code, suspect submission layer corruption before debugging logic

memory_items.title: Code Submission Verification
memory_items.lesson: Before executing generated code, verify the raw string contains no hidden prefix/suffix characters from template expansion
memory_items.generalization: Use explicit string validation (strip, repr, or encode check) on code payloads prior to tool invocation to catch corruption early

edits.content:
## Tool Execution Safety

### Code Submission Integrity
- Validate code strings contain only ASCII/printable characters before execution
- Reject submissions that begin with unexpected prefixes or contain invisible Unicode markers
- When encountering 'SyntaxError: invalid syntax' at line 1 repeatedly, inspect the raw code payload for corruption before modifying logic
- Use context managers and clean function wrappers without embedding markdown artifacts in executable code blocks

### Debugging Syntax Failures
- Distinguish between logic errors (line-specific) and submission errors (line 1, immediate)
- Trace execution failures through the tool invocation pipeline, not just the code itself
- Maintain clean separation between instruction formatting and actual code content
